label: LSASS High CPU, Memory, or Performance

l1: Is the high resource spike causing domain controllers or members to stop responding?
When did the performance degradation begin, and does it follow a cyclical pattern?
Are all authentication protocols experiencing delays, or is the bottleneck protocol-specific?
l2: Is an unindexed LDAP query sweep from an external application causing the LSASS thread lock?
Has a massive brute-force NTLM attack flooded the Netlogon queue parameters boundaries?
Is a faulty third-party security agent injection causing memory leak scenarios inside the process?

l1: Open Task Manager or Resource Monitor to note explicit LSASS utilization levels metrics data.
Check Directory Service logs for Event IDs 1175 or 1216 expensive query reports summaries.
Run 'netstat -ano' to detect abnormal volumes of connections targeting ports 389, 445, or 88.
l2: Activate the Active Directory Diagnostics data logs collection suite inside Performance Monitor.
Use NTDS logging parameter registry overrides to capture expensive client execution behaviors paths.
Review Process Explorer properties metrics tracking threads performance profiles details sheets.

l1: Block the source IP address executing abusive network queries via host firewalls rules.
Restart auxiliary dependent directory applications if they are flooding bad bind structures.
Apply current security updates package fixes targeting known core authentication engine leak bugs.
l2: Instruct application owners to create appropriate indices tracking custom Active Directory properties paths.
Configure NTLM restriction policies filters variables to force transition loops toward Kerberos pathways.
Disable problematic third-party credential provider library extensions via configuration registries trees.

l1: Collect Performance Monitor metric log captures spanning resource spike timelines windows.
Export Directory Service event logs filtering for long runtime lookup warning markers data.
Save text listings detailing system connections tables summaries tracking target client nodes.
l2: Create full process user memory dump files for lsass.exe using official diagnostic tools suites.
Export keys trees configuration details from HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Diagnostics.
Gather network packet frame sequences traces highlighting transaction frequencies histories files.

unindexed_ldap_query_flood.title: Isolating and Stopping High LSASS CPU Spikes Sparked by Wildcard LDAP Requests

steps.expect: Shows extreme CPU utilization rows metrics or severe continuous memory accumulation values
Extracts client IP tracking documentation and reveals the problematic unindexed search filter parameters string

steps.action: New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName 'Block Abusive Client App' -Direction Inbound -RemoteAddress '10.50.2.84' -Action Block
steps.expect: Instantly severs connection bounds, preventing the client app from transmitting successive bad queries threads
Resource utilization levels drop cleanly down below baseline target limits instantly
